name: Numan ibn Bashir
title: The Dismissed Governor
role: Governor of Kufa before the tragedy
shortDescription: The Umayyad governor of Kufa who was dismissed by Yazid for his leniency towards Muslim ibn Aqeel.
fullStory: Numan ibn Bashir was the governor of Kufa appointed by Mu'awiya and later retained by Yazid. When Muslim ibn Aqeel arrived in Kufa and the people pledged allegiance to him, Numan took a peaceful stance, avoiding bloodshed. In a speech, he declared that he would only fight those who fought him. This lenient approach prompted Umayyad loyalists to complain to Yazid. Consequently, Yazid swiftly dismissed Numan and appointed Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, the ruthless governor of Basra, to brutally crush the uprising.
quote: I will not fight anyone who does not fight me, nor will I attack anyone who does not attack me.
